name: [TEMPLATE] [gsc_csv_plugin] GSC Integration (CSV stopgap) — parse → CTR/cannibalization findings → notify

nodes.name: Write Performance

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify(($json.__rows || []).map((r) => ({ tenant: $json.__tenant, property: ($json.__property || ''), query: (r.query || ''), page: (r.page || ''), clicks: r.clicks, impressions: r.impressions, ctr: r.ctr, position: r.position, date: (r.date || '0001-01-01'), run_date: $json.__run_date }))) }}

nodes.notes: PAT-4 guard: body may be an empty HTTP 200 — never JSON.parse blind. Use: const t=(response.body??'').toString(); t.trim()?JSON.parse(t):[]

nodes.name: Write Findings

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify(($json.__findings || []).map((f) => ({ tenant: $json.__tenant, finding_id: f.finding_id, query: f.query, page: f.page, finding_class: f.finding_class, impressions: f.impressions, current_ctr: f.current_ctr, position: f.position, expected_ctr: f.expected_ctr, recommendation: f.recommendation, evidence: f.evidence, run_date: $json.__run_date }))) }}

nodes.notes: PAT-4 guard: body may be an empty HTTP 200 — never JSON.parse blind. Use: const t=(response.body??'').toString(); t.trim()?JSON.parse(t):[]

nodes.name: Notify Report
nodes.notes: PAT-4 guard: body may be an empty HTTP 200 — never JSON.parse blind. Use: const t=(response.body??'').toString(); t.trim()?JSON.parse(t):[]
